steps.title: Find the radius from the circumference
steps.content:
Given circumference $C = 20\pi$, use the formula $C = 2\pi r$:
$$20\pi = 2\pi r$$
$$r = 10 \text{ meters}$$

steps.title: Calculate the volume
steps.content:
Substitute $r = 10$ and $h = 6$ into the cylinder volume formula:
$$V = \pi(10)^2(6) = \pi \cdot 100 \cdot 6 = 600\pi \text{ cubic meters}$$
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
